NAME

log.c - 📝 Multi-level logging with terminal color support, file rotation, and async output

Data Structures


struct log_system_t

Macros

#define LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR( error, message, ...)

Value:

do {                                                                                                                 \


  asciichat_set_errno_with_message(error, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, message, ##__VA_ARGS__);                     \


  static const char *msg_header = "CRITICAL LOGGING SYSTEM ERROR: ";                                                 \


  safe_fprintf(stderr, "%s %s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, msg_header), message);                             \


  int _log_fd = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);                                                                        \


  platform_write(_log_fd, msg_header, strlen(msg_header));                                                           \


  platform_write(_log_fd, message, strlen(message));                                                                 \


  platform_write(_log_fd, "\n", 1);                                                                                  \


  backtrace_print_simple(0);                                                                                       \
} while (0)

char
  * format_message (const char * format, va_list args)


Definition at line 248 of file log/log.c.


248                                                        {
249   if (!format) {
250     return NULL;
251   }
252 
253   // First, determine the size needed
254   va_list args_copy;
255   va_copy(args_copy, args);
256   int size = safe_vsnprintf(NULL, 0, format, args_copy);
257   va_end(args_copy);
258 
259   if (size < 0) {
260     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format context message");
261     return NULL;
262   }
263 
264   // Allocate and format the message
265   char *message = SAFE_MALLOC(size + 1, char *);
266   int result = safe_vsnprintf(message, (size_t)size + 1, format, args);
267   if (result < 0) {
268     SAFE_FREE(message);
269     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format context message");
270     return NULL;
271   }
272 
273   return message;
274 }

size_t
  get_current_time_formatted (char * time_buf)


Definition at line 216 of file log/log.c.


216                                                   {
217   /* Get wall-clock time in nanoseconds */
218   uint64_t ts_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
219   // Extract seconds and nanoseconds from total nanoseconds
220   time_t seconds = (time_t)(ts_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT);
221   long nanoseconds = (long)(ts_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT);
222   struct tm tm_info;
223   platform_localtime(&seconds, &tm_info);
224   // Format the time part first
225   // strftime returns 0 on error, not negative (and len is size_t/unsigned)
226   size_t len = strftime(time_buf, 32, "%H:%M:%S", &tm_info);
227   if (len == 0 || len >= 32) {
228     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format time");
229     return 0;
230   }
231 
232   // Add microseconds manually (convert nanoseconds to microseconds for display)
233   long microseconds = nanoseconds / 1000;
234   if (microseconds < 0)
235     microseconds = 0;
236   if (microseconds > 999999)
237     microseconds = 999999;
238 
239   int result = safe_snprintf(time_buf + len, 32 - len, ".%06ld", microseconds);
240   if (result < 0 || result >= (int)(32 - len)) {
241     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format microseconds");
242     return 0;
243   }
244 
245   return len + (size_t)result;
246 }

const
  char * get_level_string_padded (log_level_t level)


Get padded level string for consistent alignment. Returns level
    names padded to 5 characters for visual alignment. Used consistently in both
    colored and plain text formatters to ensure grep pattern matching works
    correctly.




Parameters


level Log level


Returns


Padded level string (e.g., "INFO ", "WARN
  ", "DEBUG")


Definition at line 149 of file log/log.c.


149                                                        {
150   const char *result;
151   switch (level) {
152   case LOG_INFO:
153     result = "INFO "; // 5 chars: INFO + 1 space
154     break;
155   case LOG_WARN:
156     result = "WARN "; // 5 chars: WARN + 1 space
157     break;
158   case LOG_DEV:
159     result = "DEV  "; // 5 chars: DEV + 2 spaces
160     break;
161   case LOG_DEBUG:
162     result = "DEBUG"; // 5 chars: DEBUG (no padding needed)
163     break;
164   case LOG_ERROR:
165     result = "ERROR"; // 5 chars: ERROR (no padding needed)
166     break;
167   case LOG_FATAL:
168     result = "FATAL"; // 5 chars: FATAL (no padding needed)
169     break;
170   default:
171     result = "?????"; // Invalid level - return 5 question marks
172   }
173   // Verify length
174   if (strlen(result) != 5) {
175     fprintf(stderr, "ERROR: get_level_string_padded() returned non-5-char string: '%s' (len=%zu)\n", result,
176             strlen(result));
177   }
178   return result;
179 }

Definition at line 2111 of file log/log.c.


2111                                                                                                             {
2112   if (!message) {
2113     return;
2114   }
2115 
2116   int fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(level);
2117   if (fd < 0) {
2118     return;
2119   }
2120 
2121   // Check if JSON output is enabled (json_file >= 0 means enabled)
2122   int json_fd = atomic_load_int(&g_log.json_file);
2123   bool use_json = (json_fd >= 0);
2124 
2125   if (use_json) {
2126     // Use async-safe JSON formatter (safe for signal handlers)
2127     log_json_async_safe(fd, level, file, line, func, message);
2128   } else {
2129     // Text output to console using platform_write_all to handle partial writes
2130     size_t msg_len = strlen(message);
2131     platform_write_all(fd, (const uint8_t *)message, msg_len);
2132     if (msg_len == 0 || message[msg_len - 1] != '\n') {
2133       platform_write_all(fd, (const uint8_t *)"\n", 1);
2134     }
2135   }
2136 }

void
  log_destroy (void )


Definition at line 622 of file log/log.c.


622                        {
623   // Destroy mmap logging first (if active)
624   if (log_mmap_is_active()) {
625     log_mmap_destroy();
626   }
627 
628   // Cleanup grep filter
629   grep_destroy();
630 
631   /* Mark logging system as shutdown FIRST (state machine transition)
632    * This signals worker threads to stop logging immediately before we free memory.
633    * This prevents TOCTOU race where a worker thread could see non-NULL format pointer
634    * after we've freed it but before lifecycle_shutdown() is called. */
635   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log.lifecycle);
636 
637   // Cleanup custom format structures: All worker threads have been signaled to stop by this point.
638   // Safe to free templates now without use-after-free concerns.
639   if (g_log.format) {
640     log_template_free(g_log.format);
641     g_log.format = NULL;
642   }
643   if (g_log.format_console_only) {
644     log_template_free(g_log.format_console_only);
645     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
646   }
647   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.has_custom_format, false);
648 
649   // Lock-free cleanup using atomic operations
650   int old_file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
651   if (old_file >= 0 && old_file != STDERR_FILENO) {
652     platform_close(old_file);
653   }
654   atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1);
655 
656   // Destroy rotation mutex
657   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle);
658 }

const
  char ** log_get_color_array (void )


Definition at line 1620 of file log/log.c.


1620                                        {
1621   init_terminal_capabilities();
1622 
1623   /* Colors should be initialized via log_system_init(); this is just a safety check */
1624   if (!g_log.log_colorscheme_initialized) {
1625     /* Fallback: initialize colors if not already done (should not happen after log_system_init) */
1626     log_init_colors();
1627   }
1628 
1629   /* Safety check: if colors are still not initialized, return NULL to prevent crashes from null pointers */
1630   if (!g_log.log_colorscheme_initialized) {
1631     return NULL;
1632   }
1633 
1634   /* Return the compiled color scheme based on terminal capabilities
1635    * codes_16, codes_256, codes_truecolor are now proper arrays of pointers (const char *[8]),
1636    * so we can safely cast them to const char **. */
1637   if (g_log.terminal_caps.color_level >= TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR) {
1638     return (const char **)g_log.compiled_colors.codes_truecolor;
1639   } else if (g_log.terminal_caps.color_level >= TERM_COLOR_256) {
1640     return (const char **)g_log.compiled_colors.codes_256;
1641   } else {
1642     return (const char **)g_log.compiled_colors.codes_16;
1643   }
1644 }

void
  * log_get_template (void )


Get the current log format template.


Returns


Opaque pointer to the current log format template (may be
  NULL if not set)


Returns the compiled log format template used by the logging
    system. This is used by platform code (e.g., backtrace formatting) to format
    log entries using the same template as the rest of the logging system.
    Returns void* (opaque) to avoid circular dependency with format.h.


Definition at line 2147 of file log/log.c.

2148   return (void *)g_log.format;
2149 }

void
  log_init (const char * filename, log_level_t level, bool
  force_stderr, bool use_mmap)


Definition at line 532 of file log/log.c.


532                                                                                          {
533   // Initialize rotation mutex (only operation that uses a mutex)
534   g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle.sync_type = LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX;
535   g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle.sync.mutex = &g_log.rotation_mutex;
536   lifecycle_init(&g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle, "log_rotation");
537 
538   // Set basic config using atomic stores
539   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.force_stderr, force_stderr);
540   bool preserve_terminal_output = atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled);
541 
542   // Force logs to stderr if stdout is piped/redirected
543   // This prevents logs from contaminating piped/redirected output in snapshot/batch modes
544   // Per terminal.h: "force stderr when piped to prevent data corruption"
545   if (terminal_is_piped_output()) {
546     atomic_store_bool(&g_log.force_stderr, true);
547   }
548 
549   // Close any existing file (atomic load/store)
550   int old_file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
551   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle) && old_file >= 0 && old_file != STDERR_FILENO) {
552     platform_close(old_file);
553     atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1);
554   }
555 
556   // Check LOG_LEVEL environment variable
557   const char *env_level_str = SAFE_GETENV("LOG_LEVEL");
558   if (env_level_str) {
559     atomic_store_u64(&g_log.level, (int)parse_log_level_from_env());
560   } else {
561     atomic_store_u64(&g_log.level, (int)level);
562   }
563 
564   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.level_manually_set, false);
565   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.current_size, 0);
566 
567   if (filename) {
568     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_log.filename, filename, sizeof(g_log.filename) - 1);
569 
570     if (use_mmap) {
571       // Lock-free mmap path - writes go to mmap'd file
572       asciichat_error_t mmap_result = log_mmap_init_simple(filename, 0);
573       if (mmap_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
574         atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1); // No regular fd - using mmap for file output
575       } else {
576         // Mmap failed - use stderr only (atomic writes, lock-free)
577         if (preserve_terminal_output) {
578           safe_fprintf(stderr, "Mmap logging failed for %s, using stderr only (lock-free)\n", filename);
579         }
580         atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, STDERR_FILENO);
581         g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
582       }
583     } else {
584       // Lock-free file I/O path - uses atomic write() syscalls
585       int fd = platform_open("log_file", filename, O_CREAT | O_RDWR | O_TRUNC, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE);
586       atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, (fd >= 0) ? fd : STDERR_FILENO);
587       if (fd < 0) {
588         if (preserve_terminal_output) {
589           safe_fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open log file: %s\n", filename);
590         }
591         g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
592       }
593     }
594   } else {
595     atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, STDERR_FILENO);
596     g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
597   }
598 
599   /* Initialize default log format (NULL means use mode-specific default) */
600   log_set_format(NULL, false);
601 
602   /* Mark logging system as initialized (state machine transition) */
603   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle)) {
604     lifecycle_init(&g_log.lifecycle, "logging");
605   }
606 
607   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled, preserve_terminal_output);
608 
609   // Reset terminal detection if needed
610   if (g_log.terminal_caps_initialized && !g_log.terminal_caps.detection_reliable) {
611     g_log.terminal_caps_initialized = false;
612   }
613 
614   // Detect terminal capabilities

1682

size_t
  log_recolor_plain_entry (const char * plain_line, char * colored_buf, size_t
  buf_size)


Recolor a plain (non-colored) log line with proper ANSI codes.
    Parses plain log format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [tid:THREAD_ID] FILE:LINE in
    FUNC(): MESSAGE And recolors it with appropriate ANSI codes matching the
    colored format.


Definition at line 1859 of file log/log.c.


1859                                                                                            {
1860   if (!plain_line || !colored_buf || buf_size < 128) {
1861     return 0;
1862   }
1863 
1864   static char work_buffer[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE + 1024];
1865 
1866   // Parse format FIRST, regardless of color availability
1867   // Parse format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [tid:THREAD_ID] FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE
1868   const char *p = plain_line;
1869 
1870   // Extract timestamp [TIMESTAMP]
1871   if (*p != '[') {
1872     return 0; // Invalid format
1873   }
1874   p++;
1875   const char *timestamp_start = p;
1876 
1877   // Find the closing ] for timestamp - look for pattern that ends with proper timestamp format
1878   // Valid timestamp: HH:MM:SS.UUUUUU (time with microseconds, no date)
1879   while (*p && *p != ']') {
1880     p++;
1881   }
1882   if (*p != ']') {
1883     return 0; // Malformed - no closing bracket
1884   }
1885 
1886   size_t timestamp_len = p - timestamp_start;
1887   char timestamp[64];
1888   if (timestamp_len >= sizeof(timestamp) || timestamp_len == 0) {
1889     return 0; // Invalid timestamp - empty or too long
1890   }
1891   SAFE_STRNCPY(timestamp, timestamp_start, timestamp_len);
1892   timestamp[timestamp_len] = '\0';
1893   p++; // Skip ]
1894 
1895   // Skip whitespace after timestamp
1896   while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1897     p++;
1898   }
1899 
1900   // Extract level [LEVEL]
1901   if (*p != '[') {
1902     return 0; // Missing opening bracket for level
1903   }
1904   p++;
1905   const char *level_start = p;
1906   while (*p && *p != ']') {
1907     p++;
1908   }
1909   if (*p != ']') {
1910     return 0; // Missing closing bracket for level
1911   }
1912   size_t level_len = p - level_start;
1913   char level_str[16];
1914   if (level_len >= sizeof(level_str) || level_len == 0) {
1915     return 0; // Level string too long or empty
1916   }
1917   /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
1918   strncpy(level_str, level_start, level_len);
1919   level_str[level_len] = '\0';
1920 
1921   // Determine log level for color selection
1922   log_level_t level = LOG_INFO; // Default
1923   if (strstr(level_str, "DEV") || strstr(level_str, "DEBUG")) {
1924     level = LOG_DEBUG;
1925   } else if (strstr(level_str, "INFO")) {
1926     level = LOG_INFO;
1927   } else if (strstr(level_str, "WARN")) {
1928     level = LOG_WARN;
1929   } else if (strstr(level_str, "ERROR")) {
1930     level = LOG_ERROR;
1931   } else if (strstr(level_str, "FATAL")) {
1932     level = LOG_FATAL;
1933   }
1934 
1935   p++; // Skip ]
1936 
1937   // Skip whitespace after level
1938   while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1939     p++;
1940   }
1941 
1942   // Extract thread ID [tid:THREAD_ID] - optional field
1943   uint64_t tid = 0;
1944   if (*p == '[' && strncmp(p, "[tid:", 5) == 0) {
1945     p += 5;
1946     char *tid_end = NULL;
1947     tid = strtoull(p, &tid_end, 10);
1948     if (!tid_end || *tid_end != ']') {
1949       // tid parsing failed, but continue anyway (might still recover)
1950       // Try to find the closing bracket
1951       while (*p && *p != ']') {
1952         p++;
1953       }
1954       if (*p == ']') {
1955         p++;
1956       }
1957     } else {
1958       p = tid_end + 1; // Skip past the ]
1959     }
1960     // Skip whitespace after tid
1961     while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1962       p++;
1963     }
1964   }
1965   // tid is optional - continue parsing if not present
1966 
1967   // Extract file path (everything up to :LINE)
1968   const char *file_start = p;
1969   while (*p && *p != ':') {
1970     p++;
1971   }
1972   if (*p != ':') {
1973     return 0; // Malformed
1974   }
1975   size_t file_len = p - file_start;
1976   char file_path[256];
1977   if (file_len >= sizeof(file_path)) {
1978     return 0;
1979   }
1980   /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
1981   strncpy(file_path, file_start, file_len);
1982   file_path[file_len] = '\0';
1983   p++; // Skip :
1984 
1985   // Extract line number (digits only)
1986   int line_num = 0;
1987   const char *line_start = p;
1988   while (*p && isdigit((unsigned char)*p)) {
1989     p++;
1990   }
1991   if (p == line_start) {
1992     return 0; // No line number
1993   }
1994   line_num = (int)strtol(line_start, NULL, 10);
1995 
1996   // Skip whitespace and find "in" keyword (be very lenient)
1997   while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\n' || *p == '\r')) {
1998     p++;
1999   }
2000 
2001   // Try to find "in " - if not found, might still be valid, just harder to parse
2002   if (strncmp(p, "in ", 3) == 0) {
2003     p += 3; // Skip "in "
2004   } else if (*p == 'i' && *(p + 1) == 'n' && (*(p + 2) == ' ' || *(p + 2) == '\t')) {
2005     // Allow tab after "in"
2006     p += 2; // Skip "in"
2007     while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t')) {
2008       p++;
2009     }
2010   } else {
2011     // Missing "in" keyword - this is a format error
2012     return 0;
2013   }
2014 
2015   // Extract function name (everything up to "()")
2016   const char *func_start = p;
2017   while (*p && *p != '(') {
2018     p++;
2019   }
2020   if (*p != '(' || func_start == p) {
2021     return 0; // Missing function name or parentheses
2022   }
2023   size_t func_len = p - func_start;
2024 
2025   // Trim trailing whitespace from function name
2026   while (func_len > 0 && (func_start[func_len - 1] == ' ' || func_start[func_len - 1] == '\t')) {
2027     func_len--;
2028   }
2029 
2030   char func_name[256];
2031   if (func_len >= sizeof(func_name)) {
2032     func_len = sizeof(func_name) - 1;
2033   }
2034   if (func_len > 0) {
2035     /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
2036     strncpy(func_name, func_start, func_len);
2037   }
2038   func_name[func_len] = '\0';
2039 
2040   // Skip "(" and ")" - be lenient about what's between them
2041   if (*p == '(') {
2042     p++;
2043     while (*p && *p != ')') {
2044       p++;
2045     }
2046     if (*p == ')') {
2047       p++;
2048     }
2049   }
2050 
2051   // Skip whitespace and optional colon(s) and other separators
2052   while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == ':' || *p == '\t')) {
2053     p++;
2054   }
2055 
2056   // Remaining is the message
2057   const char *message = p;
2058 
2059   // Format is valid, get colors from logging system
2060   const char **colors = log_get_color_array();
2061   if (!colors) {
2062     // No colors available, return plain text
2063     static bool warned_once = false;
2064     if (!warned_once) {
2065       log_debug("WARNING: log_recolor_plain_entry() called but colors not initialized - returning plain text");
2066       warned_once = true;
2067     }
2068     size_t len = strlen(plain_line);
2069     if (len >= buf_size) {
2070       return 0;
2071     }
2072     SAFE_STRNCPY(colored_buf, plain_line, buf_size - 1);
2073     colored_buf[buf_size - 1] = '\0';
2074     return len;
2075   }
2076 
2077   // Build colored output
2078   const char *level_color = colors[level];
2079   const char *reset = colors[LOG_COLOR_RESET];
2080   const char *file_color = colors[1]; // DEBUG/Cyan
2081   const char *line_color = colors[6]; // GREY (matching tid)
2082   const char *func_color = colors[0]; // DEV/Orange
2083   const char *tid_color = colors[6];  // GREY
2084 
2085   int len = safe_snprintf(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer),
2086                           "[%s%s%s] [%s%s%s] [tid:%s%llu%s] %s%s%s:%s%d%s in %s%s%s(): %s", level_color, timestamp,
2087                           reset, level_color, level_str, reset, tid_color, (unsigned long long)tid, reset, file_color,
2088                           file_path, reset, line_color, line_num, reset, func_color, func_name, reset, message);
2089 
2090   if (len <= 0 || len >= (int)sizeof(work_buffer)) {
2091     return 0;
2092   }
2093 
2094   // Colorize the message part
2095   const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(message);
2096   len = safe_snprintf(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer),
2097                       "[%s%s%s] [%s%s%s] [tid:%s%llu%s] %s%s%s:%s%d%s in %s%s%s(): %s", level_color, timestamp, reset,
2098                       level_color, level_str, reset, tid_color, (unsigned long long)tid, reset, file_color, file_path,
2099                       reset, line_color, line_num, reset, func_color, func_name, reset, colorized_msg);
2100 
2101   if (len <= 0 || len >= (int)sizeof(work_buffer) || len >= (int)buf_size) {
2102     return 0;
2103   }
2104 
2105   SAFE_STRNCPY(colored_buf, work_buffer, buf_size - 1);
2106   colored_buf[buf_size - 1] = '\0';
2107   return (size_t)len;
2108 }






References colorize_log_message(), file_path,
    log_get_color_array(), and safe_snprintf().






void
  log_redetect_terminal_capabilities (void )

1594                                               {
1595   // Guard against recursion
1596   if (g_log.terminal_caps_detecting) {
1597     return;
1598   }
1599 
1600   // Detect if not initialized, or if we're using defaults (not reliably detected)
1601   // This ensures we get proper detection after logging is ready, replacing any defaults
1602   // Once we have reliable detection, never re-detect to keep colors consistent
1603   if (!g_log.terminal_caps_initialized || !g_log.terminal_caps.detection_reliable) {
1604     g_log.terminal_caps_detecting = true;
1605     g_log.terminal_caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
1606     g_log.terminal_caps_detecting = false;
1607     g_log.terminal_caps_initialized = true;
1608 
1609     // Now log the capabilities AFTER colors are set, so this log uses the correct colors
1610     log_debug("Terminal capabilities: color_level=%d, capabilities=0x%x, utf8=%s, fps=%d",
1611               g_log.terminal_caps.color_level, g_log.terminal_caps.capabilities,
1612               g_log.terminal_caps.utf8_support ? "yes" : "no", g_log.terminal_caps.desired_fps);
1613 
1614     // Now that we've detected once with reliable results, keep these colors consistent for all future logs
1615   }

void

Definition at line 697 of file log/log.c.


697                                            {
698   // Respect --quiet flag: if quiet is set, never enable terminal output
699   // But allow disabling even if options are unavailable (for shutdown cleanup)
700 
701   // Only check quiet flag when ENABLING output to avoid deadlock with
702   // concurrent logging from other threads (options_get() can deadlock when
703   // called while other threads are actively logging due to RCU synchronization)
704   if (enabled) {
705     const options_t *opts = options_get();
706     if (opts && opts->quiet) {
707       return; // Silently ignore attempts to enable terminal output when --quiet is set
708     }
709   }
710 
711   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled, enabled);
712 }

void
  log_system_destroy (void )


Shutdown the logging system internal state (system-level cleanup).
    Clears registered session log buffer. Color cleanup is handled separately in
    asciichat_shared_destroy() after memory reporting. Must be called
    once at program shutdown AFTER log_destroy(). Safe to call multiple
    times (idempotent).

void
  log_system_init (void )


Initialize the logging system internal state (system-level init).

    Safe to call multiple times (idempotent).
